title_zh: MigMate：一个基于LLM的Python项目库迁移的VS Code扩展

title: AI-based Tools Test Cases Generation and Software Correctness: A Critical Review
authors: Sara Abu Dabaa’t, MohammadNoor Injadat, Ahmad Samhan
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is now increasingly transforming software quality assurance, and test case generation is among its most notable use cases. Here, this study provides a review of AI-based techniques and tools facilitating automated test case generation, evidencing their effectiveness in verifying software correctness. From over fifty of the latest academic and industrial contributions published from 2020 to 2025, machine learning models, natural language processing approaches, symbolic execution techniques, and generative AI models have been reviewed. The review identifies key accomplishments of AI approaches, including improvements in test coverage of up to 60%, correction of up to 30% of post-deployment defects, and significant testing cycle reduction. However, challenges persist in aspects of data quality, model interpretability, deployment in continuous development pipelines, and the balancing of automation and manual intervention. Due to these gaps, the study introduces a comparative overview of testing strategies with a focus on AI and reveal the demand for empirical validation, standardized tests, and hybrid solutions that combine AI automation and expert knowledge. The findings demonstrate the revolutionary effect of AI on current software testing and reveal important milestones to be attained for its fruitful use in research and practice.
title_zh: 基于AI的工具的测试用例生成与软件正确性：一项批判性综述
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）正日益深刻地变革软件质量保障，而测试用例生成是其最显著的应用之一。本研究综述了基于AI的技术与工具如何促进自动化测试用例生成，并证明其在验证软件正确性方面的有效性。通过分析2020至2025年间发表的五十余项最新学术与工业成果，我们评估了机器学习模型、自然语言处理方法、符号执行技术及生成式AI模型。综述指出AI方法的关键成就包括：测试覆盖率提升高达60%、修复多达30%的部署后缺陷，以及显著缩短测试周期。然而，在数据质量、模型可解释性、持续开发流程中的部署，以及自动化与人工干预的平衡方面仍存在挑战。针对这些不足，本研究引入了以AI为重点的测试策略比较概述，揭示了实证验证、标准化测试及结合AI自动化与专家知识的混合解决方案的需求。研究结果展示了AI对当前软件测试的革命性影响，并指出了其在研究与实践中有成效应用所需达到的重要里程碑。

title: LLM-Seeded Genetic Improvement: Hierarchical AST Evolution with Multi-Level Donor-Code Transplants
authors: Michael Schladt, John C. Gallagher
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
abstract: Automated software transformation remains a key challenge in search-based software engineering, particularly when targeting high-level behavioral changes. We present a novel genetic improvement (GI) framework that evolves program source code using a novel hierarchical abstract syntax tree (AST) representation and a suite of specialized genetic operators. Our approach supports multilevel structural edits, including insertion, deletion, and replacement of AST subtrees, and enables code transplants from a pool of LLM-generated donor programs. Donor code is generated automatically by prompting a large language model (LLM) to solve the target task in diverse ways, then extracting and preserving every syntactically valid AST node from its outputs. Candidate variants are evaluated using a multi-stage fitness function that prioritizes compatibility, functional correctness, and behavioral alignment with a specified target. As a proof-of-concept, we evolve a breadth-first search implementation into a depth-first search solely through automated AST-based edits. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach efficiently discovers minimal and correct transformations, underscoring the importance of both representation and operator design in automated software evolution. Our results broaden GI’s capabilities toward domain-agnostic, behavior-changing code evolution. This work lays a foundation for more scalable automated software adaptation and repair techniques.
title_zh: LLM初始化的遗传改进：基于分层抽象语法树演化与多级捐赠代码移植
abstract_zh: 自动化软件变换仍然是基于搜索的软件工程中的一项关键挑战，尤其是在实现高层行为变更方面。我们提出了一种新颖的遗传改进框架，该框架通过一种分层抽象语法树表示方法和一套专门的遗传算子来演进程序源代码。该方法支持多级结构编辑，包括AST子树的插入、删除和替换，并能从大语言模型生成的供体程序池中实现代码移植。供体代码通过提示大语言模型以多种方式解决目标任务自动生成，然后提取并保留其输出中所有语法有效的AST节点。候选变体通过一个多阶段适应度函数进行评估，该函数优先考虑兼容性、功能正确性以及与指定目标的行为对齐。作为概念验证，我们仅通过基于AST的自动编辑就将广度优先搜索实现演进为深度优先搜索。实验结果表明，我们的方法能够高效地发现最小且正确的变换，凸显了表示和算子设计在自动化软件演进中的重要性。我们的研究结果将GI的能力扩展到领域无关、行为变更的代码演进，为更具可扩展性的自动化软件适应和修复技术奠定了基础。

title: When Many-Shot Prompting Fails: An Empirical Study of LLM Code Translation
authors: Amirkia Rafiei Oskooei, Kaan Baturalp Cosdan, Husamettin Isiktas, Mehmet S. Aktas
publish: Proceedings of the 1st Workshop on Code Translation, Transformation, and Modernization
title_zh: 当多示例提示失效时：LLM代码翻译的实证研究

title: Prompt Optimization through Reinforcement Learning for Generative Language Model Code Synthesis in Multi-Robot Systems
authors: Kaushik Kannan, Jungyun Bae
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: In multi-robot systems (MRS) operating across various applications, real-time task allocation and path planning pose significant challenges, often requiring extensive human intervention under extreme time constraints. This paper introduces a novel framework that leverages Reinforcement Learning (RL) to automate and optimize the code generation process for MRS. Our approach trains an RL agent to dynamically generate optimized prompts for a Large Language Model (LLM). By refining prompt structures, templates, and parameters, the RL agent guides the LLM to produce efficient, feasible, and directly executable code for complex task allocation and path-planning problems. We demonstrate that the generated solutions are both complete and high-performing. The proposed method is evaluated on a sample case of search-and-rescue scenarios, using GPT-4.1 as the LLM, and demonstrates significant performance improvements over previous work that used manual prompt optimization for similar applications. This work represents a significant step towards autonomous multi-robot coordination in time-critical dynamic environments, reducing human workload and improving mission efficacy.
title_zh: 基于强化学习的多机器人系统中生成式语言模型代码合成的提示优化
abstract_zh: 在多机器人系统（MRS）运行于各类应用中时，实时任务分配与路径规划面临重大挑战，往往需要在极端时间约束下依赖大量人工干预。本文提出了一种新颖框架，利用强化学习（RL）自动化并优化MRS的代码生成过程。我们的方法训练一个RL智能体，以动态生成针对大型语言模型（LLM）的优化提示。通过改进提示结构、模板和参数，该RL智能体引导LLM生成高效、可行且可直接执行的代码，以解决复杂的任务分配与路径规划问题。我们证明，生成的解决方案既完整又高性能。本方法在搜索救援场景的示例案例中进行了评估，使用GPT-4.1作为LLM，并展现出相较于先前在类似应用中采用手动提示优化的研究工作，性能有显著提升。这项工作向在时间紧迫的动态环境中实现自主多机器人协调迈出了重要一步，减轻了人类工作负担并提高了任务效能。

title: Asm2Src-LLMEval: A GenAI Pipeline for Multi-Metric Assessment of LLMs in Program Translation and Analysis
authors: Parisa Hamedi, Hamed Jelodar, Samita Bai, Mohammad Meymani, Roozbeh Razavi-Far, Ali Ghorbani
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
abstract: Assembly-to-source code translation is a critical task in reverse engineering, cybersecurity, and software maintenance, yet systematic benchmarks for evaluating large language models on this problem remain scarce. In this work, we present the first comprehensive evaluation of five state-of-the-art large language models on assembly-to-source translation. We assess model performance using a diverse set of metrics capturing lexical similarity (BLEU, ROUGE, and METEOR), semantic alignment (BERTScore), fluency (Perplexity), and efficiency (time prediction). Our results reveal clear trade-offs: while certain models excel in text similarity metrics, others demonstrate lower perplexity or faster inference times. We further provide qualitative analyses of typical model successes and failure cases, highlighting challenges such as control flow recovery and identifier reconstruction. Taken together, our benchmark offers actionable insights into the strengths and limitations of current large language models for program translation, establishing a foundation for future research in combining accuracy with efficiency for real-world applications..
title_zh: Asm2Src-LLMEval：一种面向程序翻译与分析中LLM多指标评估的GenAI流水线
abstract_zh: 汇编到源代码的翻译是逆向工程、网络安全和软件维护中的一项关键任务，然而针对这一问题的系统性基准测试仍然稀缺，难以评估大型语言模型在该任务上的表现。在本工作中，我们首次对五种最先进的大型语言模型在汇编到源代码翻译任务上进行了全面评估。我们采用一组多样化的指标来评估模型性能，涵盖词汇相似性（BLEU、ROUGE和METEOR）、语义对齐（BERTScore）、流畅性（困惑度）以及效率（预测时间）。结果揭示了明显的权衡：某些模型在文本相似性指标上表现优异，而另一些则在低困惑度或更快推理速度上更胜一筹。我们进一步提供了典型成功与失败案例的定性分析，突显了控制流恢复和标识符重构等挑战。总体而言，我们的基准测试为当前大型语言模型在程序翻译方面的优势与局限提供了可操作的见解，为未来在真实应用中结合准确性与效率的研究奠定了基础。

title: OGD4All: A Framework for Accessible Interaction with Geospatial Open Government Data Based on Large Language Models
authors: Michael Siebenmann, Javier Argota Sánchez-Vaquerizo, Stefan Arisona, Krystian Samp, Luis Gisler, Dirk Helbing
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
abstract: We present OGD4All, a transparent, auditable, and reproducible framework based on Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance citizens’ interaction with geospatial Open Government Data (OGD). The system combines semantic data retrieval, agentic reasoning for iterative code generation, and secure sandboxed execution that produces verifiable multimodal outputs. Evaluated on a 199-question benchmark covering both factual and unanswerable questions, across 430 City-of-Zurich datasets and 11 LLMs, OGD4All reaches 98% analytical correctness and 94% recall while reliably rejecting questions unsupported by available data, which minimizes hallucination risks. Statistical robustness tests, as well as expert feedback, show reliability and social relevance. The proposed approach shows how LLMs can provide explainable, multimodal access to public data, advancing trustworthy AI for open governance.
title_zh: OGD4All：基于大型语言模型的地理空间开放政府数据无障碍交互框架
abstract_zh: 我们提出了OGD4All，这是一个基于大语言模型的透明、可审计且可复现的框架，旨在提升公民与地理空间开放政府数据（OGD）的交互能力。该系统结合了语义数据检索、用于迭代代码生成的自主推理，以及安全的沙盒执行环境，可生成可验证的多模态输出。该框架在包含199个问题的基准测试中进行了评估（涵盖事实性问题和不可回答的问题），涉及430个苏黎世市数据集和11种大语言模型。结果显示，OGD4All实现了98%的分析正确率和94%的召回率，同时能可靠地拒绝现有数据不支持的问题，从而最大程度降低幻觉风险。统计稳健性测试与专家反馈均证实了其可靠性和社会相关性。所提出的方法展示了大语言模型如何为公共数据提供可解释的多模态访问，从而推动开放治理中的可信人工智能发展。

title: SmartDoc: A Context-Aware Agentic Method Comment Generation Plugin
authors: Gregorio Robles, Vahid Etemadi
publish: Proceedings of the 3rd ACM/IEEE International Workshop on Integrated Development Environments
title_zh: SmartDoc：一种上下文感知的智能代理方法注释生成插件

title: Feasibility Study of Programming Compliant Bimanual Tasks with Reasoning LLMs
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Advanced Robotics and its Social Impacts (ARSO)
abstract: Programming bimanual robot manipulation in contact-aware environments is a complex process, requiring the simultaneous tuning of trajectories, coordination strategies, and impedance parameters. This feasibility study investigates the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) with reasoning capabilities to automate this workflow, moving beyond high-level planning to direct low-level controller parameterization. This approach is validated on two setups: Softbank's Pepper robot (hardware) and a dual-arm Franka FR3 system (simulation). It is demonstrated that reasoning models can generate a unified control policy, encompassing Cartesian trajectories, impedance gains, and coupling stiffness, from natural language instructions. This work provides a framework for reasoning-based code generation in compliant manipulation, feasibility results on physical hardware, and the first implementation of such compliant tasks on the Pepper robot.
title_zh: 使用推理大语言模型进行柔顺双臂任务编程的可行性研究
abstract_zh: 编程双臂机器人在接触感知环境中的操作是一个复杂过程，需要同时调整轨迹、协调策略和阻抗参数。本可行性研究探究了具有推理能力的大语言模型自动化该工作流程的潜力，不仅超越高层规划，更直接进行低层控制器参数化。该方法在两个平台上得到验证：软银Pepper机器人（硬件）和双臂Franka FR3系统（仿真）。结果表明，推理模型能够根据自然语言指令生成统一控制策略，涵盖笛卡尔轨迹、阻抗增益和耦合刚度。这项工作为基于推理的柔性操作代码生成提供了框架，展示了物理硬件上的可行性结果，并在Pepper机器人上首次实现了此类柔性任务。

title: From LLM Reasoning to Autonomous AI Agents: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Mohamed Amine Ferrag, Norbert Tihanyi, Merouane Debbah
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Large language models and autonomous AI agents have evolved rapidly, resulting in a diverse array of evaluation benchmarks, frameworks, and collaboration protocols. Driven by the growing need for standardized evaluation and integration, we systematically consolidate these fragmented efforts into a unified framework. However, the landscape remains fragmented and lacks a unified taxonomy or comprehensive survey. Therefore, we present a side-by-side comparison of benchmarks developed between 2019 and 2025 that evaluate these models and agents across multiple domains. In addition, we propose a taxonomy of approximately 60 benchmarks that cover general and academic knowledge reasoning, mathematical problem-solving, code generation and software engineering, factual grounding and retrieval, domain-specific evaluations, multimodal and embodied tasks, task orchestration, and interactive assessments. Furthermore, we review AI-agent frameworks introduced between 2023 and 2025 that integrate large language models with modular toolkits to enable autonomous decision-making and multi-step reasoning. Moreover, we present real-world applications of autonomous AI agents in materials science, biomedical research, academic ideation, software engineering, synthetic data generation, chemical reasoning, mathematical problem-solving, geographic information systems, multimedia, healthcare, and finance. We then survey key agent-to-agent collaboration protocols, namely the Agent Communication Protocol (ACP), the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and the Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A). Finally, we discuss recommendations for future research, focusing on advanced reasoning strategies, failure modes in multi-agent LLM systems, automated scientific discovery, dynamic tool integration via reinforcement learning, integrated search capabilities, and security vulnerabilities in agent protocols.
title_zh: 从大语言模型推理到自主人工智能智能体：综合评述
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型和自主AI智能体发展迅猛，催生了多样化的评估基准、框架和协作协议。在标准化评估与集成需求日益增长的驱动下，我们系统性地将这些碎片化的工作整合为一个统一框架。然而，该领域仍缺乏统一的分类体系或全面的综述。为此，我们呈现了2019年至2025年间开发的基准测试的并列比较，这些基准用于评估跨多个领域的模型和智能体。此外，我们提出了一个包含约60个基准的分类体系，涵盖通用与学术知识推理、数学问题求解、代码生成与软件工程、事实基础与检索、领域特定评估、多模态与具身任务、任务编排及交互式评估。进而，我们回顾了2023年至2025年间引入的AI智能体框架，这些框架将大型语言模型与模块化工具包相结合，以实现自主决策和多步推理。同时，我们展示了自主AI智能体在材料科学、生物医学研究、学术构思、软件工程、合成数据生成、化学推理、数学问题求解、地理信息系统、多媒体、医疗保健和金融等领域的实际应用。接着，我们调研了关键的智能体间协作协议，即智能体通信协议（ACP）、模型上下文协议（MCP）和智能体间协议（A2A）。最后，我们讨论了未来研究的建议，重点关注高级推理策略、多智能体LLM系统中的故障模式、自动化科学发现、通过强化学习实现的动态工具集成、集成搜索能力以及智能体协议中的安全漏洞。

title: Predictive Artificial Potential Fields for Energy Efficient 3D UAV Navigation
authors: Gurmeet Kaur, Kunwar Pal, Avinash Kumar Jha
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Device Intelligence, Computing and Communication Technologies (DICCT)
abstract: The insufficient onboard energy remains a persistent hindrance for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), particularly when operating in dense three dimensional areas and without guidance. The use of classical Artificial Potential Field (APF) methods for local planning is characterized by frequent oscillatory motion and abrupt heading changes, which consumes significant energy. The paper describes an energy aware local path planning framework for UAVs that utilizes a three dimensional extension of the Predictive Artificial Potential Field (PAPF). Rather than traditional APF, the proposed technique employs short horizon trajectory prediction, temporary goal guidance, and a three dimensional top quark mechanism to proactively bypass local minimals. Embedded in the planning process is an explicit energy consumption model that takes into account translational velocity and yaw rate dynamics, leading to smooth, stable, and dynamically feasible trajectories. Extensive simulations in environments with cuboid obstacles demonstrate that the proposed approach surpasses standard APF in terms of path smoothness, heading stability, and cumulative energy consumption. The outcomes demonstrate the efficiency of predictive potential field-based planning for energy limited UAV navigation in complex three dimensional domains.
title_zh: 节能三维无人机导航的预测性人工势场
abstract_zh: 机载能量不足一直是无人机（UAV）的持续障碍，尤其是在无引导的密集三维区域作业时。经典人工势场（APF）方法用于局部规划时，常出现频繁的振荡运动和突然的航向变化，导致能量大量消耗。本文提出了一种面向无人机的能量感知局部路径规划框架，该框架采用三维扩展的预测性人工势场（PAPF）。与传统APF不同，该技术通过短时轨迹预测、临时目标引导以及三维顶夸克机制，主动规避局部极小值点。规划过程中嵌入了一个显式能量消耗模型，该模型综合考虑了平移速度与偏航角速率动态特性，从而生成平滑、稳定且动态可行的轨迹。在含长方体障碍物的环境中进行的广泛仿真表明，所提方法在路径平滑度、航向稳定性及累计能量消耗方面均优于标准APF。结果显示，基于预测势场的规划方法能够有效支持能量受限的无人机在复杂三维环境中的导航。

title_zh: SafeTrans：借助LLM从C到Rust的代码转换
